nativeRange.habitat: Sandy reef-rubble zones and soft substrates; inhabits empty worm tubes

decorPreferences: live rock with crevices and tunnels

diet.notes: Has been documented eating mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, frozen meaty preparations, and other small protein-rich foods. Generally tends to accept frozen and prepared marine meaty items without significant difficulty.

avoidWith: fish that compete for tube territories

summary: A small, charismatic Eastern Pacific blenny known for occupying empty worm tubes in reef rubble. Males display a vivid orange throat and have been observed performing dramatic territorial displays; generally considered reef-safe and beginner-friendly.
careNotes: The Orangethroat Pikeblenny is commonly found in sandy reef-rubble zones from the Gulf of California to southern California, where it has been reported inhabiting empty polychaete worm tubes. Providing appropriate tube substitutes (sections of PVC pipe up to ~1.3 cm diameter, or live rock with suitably sized crevices) tends to greatly reduce stress and encourages natural territorial display behavior. While generally regarded as a peaceful community fish, individuals have been known to jealously defend their chosen tube or burrow — multiple individuals in a small tank may show aggression over territories. Males exhibit vivid sexual dichromatism: males display a bright orange throat stripe and bold markings used in dramatic tail-standing and gill-flaring displays, while females tend to show muted mottled earth tones. A tightly-fitted or weighted aquarium lid is strongly advised, as blennies generally have a reputation for jumping. Temperature range 17–28°C (averaging around 22°C) reflects the subtropical Eastern Pacific origin, so the species can tolerate somewhat cooler water than typical tropical reef fish. Secondary sources attempted: Reef2Reef wiki (403), BlueZoo Aquatics (403), Fishkeeping World (404), TFH Magazine (404) — data confirmed from LiveAquaria (primary) and FishBase (secondary).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (15.0 cm TL matches LiveAquaria's 6 in / 15.24 cm), temperature range (15.8–28°C vs 17.8–27.8°C from LA — averaged to 17–28°C), taxonomy (family Chaenopsidae, order Blenniiformes), and native range (Eastern Pacific, Gulf of California to southern California). FishBase did not provide pH, salinity, or tank-size data.
